name: Varginha Incident
description: The Varginha Incident refers to a series of events in January 1996 in Varginha, Minas Gerais, Brazil, involving the alleged capture of an extraterrestrial being by Brazilian authorities. Proponents claim that several witnesses, including three young women, encountered a strange creature with brown skin, large head, and big red eyes. They report that the creature was subsequently captured by the military. The incident quickly gained international attention, fueling speculation about a possible government cover-up. Witnesses reported seeing unusual military activity and the transportation of strange containers. Some accounts claim that the creature was injured and later died in military custody. Mainstream explanations attribute the sightings to misidentification of a mentally ill homeless man known locally as "Mudinho," combined with heightened public interest in UFOs at the time. The Brazilian military has consistently denied any involvement in capturing an extraterrestrial being. Skeptics point to inconsistencies in witness testimonies and the lack of concrete physical evidence to support the claims. The incident remains a significant case in ufology, sparking ongoing debate and investigation, although lacking verifiable proof.
subtitle: Alleged Alien Capture in Brazil

claims.claim: An extraterrestrial being was captured by Brazilian authorities.
claims.evidence: Multiple witness accounts describing a strange creature with specific features.
claims.criticism: Witness testimonies are inconsistent and potentially influenced by media coverage. The description closely resembles that of a known local individual.

claims.claim: The Brazilian military was involved in the capture and subsequent cover-up.
claims.evidence: Reports of increased military activity and transportation of unusual containers.
claims.criticism: The military denies any involvement, and explanations exist for military presence unrelated to alleged alien capture.

claims.claim: The captured creature was injured and later died in military custody.
claims.evidence: Unsubstantiated rumors and claims without verifiable evidence.
claims.criticism: No concrete evidence, such as medical records or autopsy reports, supports this claim. It relies solely on anecdotal accounts.

keyFigures.name: Vitório Pacaccini
keyFigures.role: Ufologist who extensively investigated the incident

keyFigures.name: Marco Petit
keyFigures.role: Ufologist who investigated and promoted the incident

keyFigures.name: General Sérgio Coelho
keyFigures.role: Military figure who denied military involvement

mythologyConnections: Possible parallels to cryptid sightings and folklore about strange creatures.
Modern interpretation of encounters with the unknown

timeline.event: Alleged sightings of the creature and subsequent military activity.
Ongoing investigations, debates, and media coverage of the incident.
